← the Atlas

Remote terminals over SSH

Features·budding·accepted·JUN 23, 2026

The skeleton plan for kolu#951 — remote terminals over SSH, as one R1–R10 phase tree. R1–R4 shipped (foundation · local kaval survivor · the remote-ssh spike · the standalone pulam awareness daemon), R6 shipped (the @kolu/terminal-workspace library — one fs/git impl, two homes) and R7 shipped (mirrorRemoteSurface is now a total dual, procedures forwarded); the remaining work R5 · R8 (compose one surface, the fold dissolves) · R9 (dial remotes) · R10 (canvas) is a linear roadmap defined in this note. Build logs live in the child notes — pty-daemon (R2), kaval-sessions (R3), pulam (R4).

Contents

  • User-facing description
  • Architecture-level changes
  • Implementation details
    • R5 — overflow-recovery loop
    • R-dock-unify — kolu’s Dock reads the shared agentProjection
    • R-pip-unify — one status pip across the web surfaces
    • R6 — @kolu/terminal-workspace: one library, one fs/git impl, two homes
    • R7 — mirrorRemoteSurface is now a total dual — shipped
    • R8 — compose terminal-workspace into kolu-server (the fold vanishes)
    • R9 — kolu dials remotes
    • R10 — canvas multiplex + validate
  • History

The skeleton plan for #951 — remote terminals over SSH. Every host — this machine and each ssh remote — runs the same kaval daemon; kolu-server keys its terminal endpoint by hostId, and you switch hosts from the ChromeBar, kinda like tmux sessions. R1–R4, R6 and R7 are shipped — the foundation, the local kaval survivor, the remote-ssh spike, the standalone pulam awareness daemon, @kolu/terminal-workspace (one fs/git impl, two homes; the surface is pulam’s until R8), and the total-dual surface mirror (procedures forwarded). What remains (R5 · R8 · R9) is one linear roadmap, defined in this note: harden reconnect, dial remotes and wire those into the tile, then multiplex the canvas. The shipped branches’ build logs live in the child notes: pty-daemon (R2), kaval-sessions (R3), pulam (R4).

User-facing description

kolu
hostslocalnix@prodstaging+ host⟳ live
local
kolu· master
◜codexworking
nix@prodssh
kolu· feat/dial-ssh#1412 ✓
●claudeawaiting you
nix@prodssh
infra· deploy
◜—working
local
notes· main
○claudeidle

You switch hosts from the ChromeBar — kinda like tmux sessions — and a remote host’s terminals sit on the canvas beside your local ones. A remote tile wears an ssh badge and carries the same live awareness a local tile does — git branch/dirty, PR status, agent state, and a green dot when it’s moving bytes — and it survives a network blip or a kolu-server deploy with full scrollback. One local host is always present; remotes are added on demand, auto-detected from your ssh config.

Architecture-level changes

One endpoint, bound to a dialed kaval. A kaval is a daemon you dial — local over a unix socket, remote over ssh — so remote is not a second backend. There is one TerminalEndpoint, bound to a HostLocation-resolved kaval endpoint ({kind:"local"} today, {kind:"remote",host} in R9). That resolver is the sole place a tile maps to its kaval; everything downstream talks to the backend and never asks “which kind?”. No RemoteTerminalEndpoint — R9 adds the ssh driver as an additive sibling of R2’s local driver, never a retrofit.

Two volatility axes stay open (R1/R2 closed the rest):

Axis — changes for its own reason Encapsulated by
Where a terminal’s state lives — this machine vs an ssh host the HostLocation-resolved kaval endpoint — one impl + a discriminator, not a second backend
How the backend reaches its agent — transport · framing · reconnect HostSession + @kolu/surface/links/stdio — unix socket today, ssh in R9

Zero domain knowledge crosses the transport (Hickey’s cut): the agent runs the unmodified sensors and the boundary ships their values. An earlier per-domain design (RemoteGitInfoProvider, …) was transport adapters wearing domain costumes; the single-endpoint seam dissolved them.

The awareness 'fold' was never domain logic — R8 deletes it

Through R7, remote awareness was folded server-side into the co-owned terminalMetadata record, split across a persisted/live fence (pr·agent·foreground live; cwd·git·… persisted) whose only job was keeping the ~150 ms agent firehose off the session autosave. Both were artifacts of one co-owned record. R8 gives observation its own home: the sensors write terminalWorkspaceSurface.awareness, and kolu (and the browser) read it through one seam (awarenessFor) instead of folding a copy in. One writer per fact — so the fold, the co-owned record, and the fence all vanish (the firehose never touches kolu). Detail + trade-offs in R8.

Already paid for, so R5–R10 are narrow. @kolu/surface-nix-host ships HostSession, provisionAgent (ships the derivation — a darwin parent drives a linux remote with no cross-builder), and resolveSystem; @kolu/surface ships mirrorRemoteSurface (now a total dual). R2 already shipped the host-count-agnostic spine: @kolu/surface-daemon-supervisor and the per-connect system.version handshake. So R9 is an ssh driver behind shipped seams; R8 composes one surface (the fold dissolves); R7 completed the surface mirror (#1505); R6 grew pulam’s fs/git (#1506); R5 is one wire signal.

Implementation details

The whole epic as one tree — bird’s-eye, with each row’s right-hand link a drill-down to where that phase lives (the child notes for shipped branches, the ### sections below for the rest). R1–R4 (incl. pulam-web’s framework) · R6 · R7 are shipped; R5 · R8–R10 remain.

ROADMAP — remote terminals over ssh (#951)✓ shipped · ▶ do next · ◐ build-clean · ○ todo · → drill in
✓R1 · Foundation — seam · framework · engine#981·984·1004
✓R2 · kaval — local PTY survival→ pty-daemon
✓R2.1–R2.5 · inversion → door → survival → inventory#1292…1458
✓R3 · remote-ssh spike→ kaval-sessions
✓R3.1–R3.6 · kaval-tui dials local + ssh#1364…1378
✓R4 · pulam — the workspace daemon→ pulam
✓R4.1–R4.5 · sensors → daemon → --host → fleet#1413…1497
✓R4.6 · rename arivu → pulam#1512
✓R4.7 · live git status in pulam-tui#1519
◐R4.8 · pulam-web — the browser twin→ pulam-web
✓R-pulamweb-1 · drishti reactive consumerdrishti#72
✓R-pulamweb-2 · framework — provision · fan-out · list#1524
✓R-pulamweb-3 · agent dashboard — agents by state#1535
▶R-pulamweb-4 · git status — dirty cell + drill-in (live file tree)◀ DO NEXT · gates R-compose-2
○R5 · Overflow-recovery loop (independent — any time)defined ↓
✓R-dock-unify · kolu's Dock reads the shared agentProjection (consistency)#1541
✓R-pip-unify · one status pip — Dock + dashboard render the same icon#1551
✓R6 · terminal-workspace — one fs/git impl, two homes#1506
✓R7 · mirrorRemoteSurface → total dual#1505
○R8 · compose terminal-workspace into kolu-server (the fold vanishes)defined ↓
◐R-compose-1 · awareness — kolu reads (validated green in #1510)build clean ↓
○R-compose-2 · git-status over ws (needs the file-tree repaint fix)needs R-pulamweb-4 ↓
○R9 · kolu dials remotesblocked-on R-compose-2 ↓
○R10 · canvas multiplex + validatedefined ↓

Everything marked ✓ is shipped (PRs linked). pulam-web’s agent dashboard (R-pulamweb-3) just shipped (#1535) — every agent across the fleet, by state, a render layer on already-shipped data. The live edge is now R-pulamweb-4 — the live git status (the per-agent dirty/clean cell + the file-tree drill-in), the renderer proof R-compose-2 actually needs. In parallel: R5 (overflow) is independent, and R-compose-1 (the awareness compose) is a clean build off the green #1510 reference; R9 is blocked-on R-compose-2; R10 needs R9. R-dock-unify has shipped (#1541) — kolu’s Dock is now the third consumer of the shared agentProjection (one source for the semantics) — and R-pip-unify has shipped too (#1551), making the visuals one source: the Dock and dashboard render the same StatePip from one leaf. Each row drills into its detail — the child notes for shipped branches, the ### sections below for the rest.

R5 — overflow-recovery loop

R5· overflow-recovery loop○ todo

Independent — needs nothing, blocks nothing; landable any time.

~130 LoC w/ tests; the next step. kaval sheds a slow attach subscriber by ending its iterator, today indistinguishable on the wire from a PTY exit, so the client treats the drop as terminal and freezes scrollback (onOverflow exists but is unwired). Thread a typed overflow → re-attach signal through the attach contract so the client re-attaches for a fresh snapshot (reusing the existing onRetry reset), distinct from PTY-exit. Ships independently of the remote work: a slow subscriber is reproducible on the local socket, so it is CI-testable today, and the fix hardens the attach contract for both transports — a clean prerequisite for the remote reconnect R8 leans on. herdr vs kolu flagged the same gap.

R-dock-unify — kolu’s Dock reads the shared agentProjection

R-dock-unify· Dock + dashboard, one source✓ shipped
needs ← R-pulamweb-3links born in #1535 · #1541

Shipped — #1541. The Dock now ranks awaiting/working/idle through the shared agentUrgency + URGENCY_RANK (its parked/unread/sleeping overlays stay Dock-local), and dockModel.paintBucket (the renamed paint fold) reads the shared agentPaintClass — so the duplicated state lists are gone (the orphaned isWorkingState predicate, plus the whole agentState.ts module, deleted with them). The one ranking change: a just-finished waiting agent now ranks idle (as on the dashboard) rather than awaiting-you — but it still paints awaiting (its tile glow lingers until it parks, a deliberately separate “rank vs paint” concern that the row pip, tile title, and rail chip now share through agentPaintClass), and the alert layer still notifies on it (“notify vs rank”, a third separate fold). The client reaches the projection through its existing kolu-common/surface door — a re-export, not a new client → terminal-workspace edge — and the cross-consumer differential test the #1535 review flagged as absent now pins Dock urgency ≡ agentProjection across every state. The fill-in also brought the two fleet mirrors along: pulam-web (then fleet.ts’s PAINT/paintClassFor) and pulam-tui (render.ts’s agentTone) now read agentPaintClass for their agent glyph/tone, so a waiting agent keeps the lingering paint across all three surfaces (the Dock + both mirrors). (The pixel sequel, R-pip-unify (#1551), then retired pulam-web’s PAINT/paintClassFor table entirely — its row now renders the Dock’s shared StatePip on the @kolu/theme palette, so that lingering dot reads the Dock’s violet rather than pulam-web’s old render-local amber.) The third fold, alertClass, is the one mirror gap left — pulam-web fleet notifications, deferred to R-pulamweb-5 — and a .apm/instructions rule (dock-fleet-mirror) now pins the three-surface contract so the next agent-state change keeps them in lockstep. The roadmap below is the plan it shipped from.

R-pulamweb-3 extracted the agent-state projection — agentBucket · agentUrgency · compareAgents · URGENCY_RANK · relativeTime — into @kolu/terminal-workspace/agentProjection as a shared source of truth, and both pulam-tui and pulam-web consume it. kolu’s own Dock does not: it still ranks and paints agents through its own parallel modules — dockRowRanking.ts (DockRowBucket / rankDockRows), pipVariant.ts, and the activity dot via useTerminalActivity. So “needs-you” is defined twice — once in the shared projection, once in the Dock — and the two can drift.

This phase makes the Dock the third consumer of the shared SoT, so the dashboard and the Dock agree by construction:

  • Share the core — the Dock’s needs-you / working / idle determination imports agentUrgency + URGENCY_RANK from agentProjection, and its paint fold imports agentPaintClass, replacing its own equivalents. @kolu/terminal-workspace is already a kolu-client dependency, so this is an import swap, not a new boundary.
  • Keep the Dock’s overlays local — parked (the staleness window) and unread (the fresh-transition signal), plus the tile/workspace ranking over TerminalMetadata, are Dock-specific and stay in dockRowRanking; they layer on top of the shared urgency, they don’t replace it.
  • Pin it with the missing differential test — add the cross-consumer test the #1535 review flagged as absent: a representative set of agent states yields the same urgency in the Dock as in agentProjection. That makes “consistent with pulam-web” structural, not prose.

Done when a given agent state produces the same urgency/bucket semantics in the Dock, pulam-tui, and pulam-web (one source); the Dock’s existing dockRowRanking tests still pass (parked/unread preserved); and the new differential test asserts the Dock’s urgency derivation === agentProjection.agentUrgency across representative states. A pure kolu-side refactor — no surface change, no new package.

R-pip-unify — one status pip across the web surfaces

R-pip-unify· Dock + dashboard render the same icon✓ shipped
needs ← R-dock-unifylinks #1551

Shipped — #1551. R-dock-unify unified the semantics (one agentProjection), but each web surface still rendered its own status icon — the Dock’s shaped StatePip (disk · spinning ring · dot · ☾) vs pulam-web’s inline ●/◜/○, and on different palettes (violet/teal vs amber/cyan). Same “defined twice → drifts” hazard, one layer up in pixels. R-pip-unify makes them render the same pip from one component:

  • The pip lifted into a shared web-UI leaf — StatePip + PipVariant move out of packages/client/src/canvas/dock/ into @kolu/solid-statepip (the way agentProjection got its home; not terminal-workspace, which stays presentation-neutral). StatePip takes a precomputed variant; the agent-paint → pip fold lives once in the leaf’s pipForPaintClass, which both the Dock’s pipVariant and pulam-web’s pipVariantFor route through — so drift is impossible by construction, not just test-pinned. Each surface keeps only its own overlays (the Dock’s unread→attention / parked; pulam-web’s structural sleeping ☾).
  • Colour converges onto a shared theme — the --color-* palette lifts into a CSS-only @kolu/theme both surfaces @import, so bg-alert/border-accent/text-fg-3 resolve identically. pulam-web is brought onto the shared theme (class="dark"), so its agent-state colour stops being a render-local amber that drifted — “your turn” now reads the Dock’s cool violet, working the same teal. The hand-rolled PAINT glyph table is deleted.
  • The TUI stays separate — pulam-tui renders terminal glyphs and can’t share a DOM component; it already shares the semantics, which is what matters there.

Done: a given agent state renders the identical pip (glyph · colour · animation) in the Dock and pulam-web from the one component — pinned by the shared pipForPaintClass + a cross-surface differential test (solid-statepip), pulam-web’s pipVariantFor test, and the Dock’s existing pipVariant.test.ts kept green; the @theme-in-@import + cross-package @source CSS pipeline verified by a real build. Landed independently of R-pulamweb-4 (a different part of pulam-web’s render).

R6 — @kolu/terminal-workspace: one library, one fs/git impl, two homes

R6· terminal-workspace — one fs/git impl, two homes✓ shipped
needs ← R4blocks → R8links #1506

Shipped — #1506. The host-side library that knows a terminal’s working directory becomes @kolu/terminal-workspace — the shipped @kolu/terminal-awareness (R4.1) grown to cover fs/git too, run in-process locally (kolu) and hosted by pulam remotely: the same two homes the sensors already proved. R6 ships one fs/git impl (createTerminalWorkspaceEndpoint), not one surface both homes already serve — the two homes re-expose that one impl through two deliberately different contract shapes:

  • awareness (collection) + activity (stream) — the derived status badge + the green-dot liveness, served on terminalWorkspaceSurface. Shipped.
  • the Code tab’s fs/git reads (listAll · readFile · getStatus · getDiff + repo/file change watching) — new in R6, lifted from kolu-server’s localEndpoint (a thin wrapper over kolu-git + serve-dir) into the shared library. kolu-server (in-process) re-exposes them on koluSurface’s value-bearing streams; pulam (remote) serves them on terminalWorkspaceSurface as procedures + {seq} pulse watcher streams a consumer requeries on.

This kills the duplication wart: without it, pulam would reimplement fs/git and kolu would carry two fs/git impls (its own local, pulam’s remote). Instead there is one — in-process locally, via pulam remotely. Only the thin fs/git wrapper moves; the terminal-endpoint orchestration around it (spawn · adopt · the registry) stays kolu’s and gets a remote impl in R9. The single shared surface both homes serve is not yet true — it arrives in R8, which composes terminalWorkspaceSurface into the one surface kolu serves the browser (the server-side fold dissolving with it); R9 then dials remotes and points that surface’s per-terminal dispatch at the mirror. Until then the two contract shapes are kept deliberately distinct. Nothing is folded into AwarenessValue — procedures were never part of it.

R7 — mirrorRemoteSurface is now a total dual — shipped #1505

R7· mirrorRemoteSurface → total dual✓ shipped
blocks → R9links #1505

mirrorRemoteSurface is the consume-side dual of implementSurface — and a dual must mirror every primitive its producer wires. implementSurface wires procedures too, but the mirror’s SurfaceSink carried only cells/collections/streams/events: a leaky half-mirror, harmless only because every surface mirrored so far had zero procedures. R7 closed the gap by kind: streaming state stays PUSH (subscribed and folded into the sink); a procedure is PULL (a local call runs on the remote and returns), so it can’t sit in the push-only sink — it comes back as a forwarding stub. The return is therefore { procedures, done }: procedures.<ns>.<verb>(input) relays to the remote (total — every procedure gets a stub, since a passthrough has no standing cost), and done is the former settle-on-link-close promise. Graft the stubs into a re-serve and serve ∘ mirror ≈ identity — the location-transparency the whole epic rests on — pinned in-repo by a hermetic unit test (implementSurface → directLink → mirror → re-serve → call). A @kolu/surface change → the drishti merge-gate.

Proven by drishti — the canonical external second consumer. drishti is the browser ↔ Bun ↔ ssh process-monitor that already mirrors per-host process surfaces (the graduation that first proved @kolu/surface is electricity), and it was read-only. R7’s external proof is drishti gaining a “Kill process” action — the first forwarded procedure on a mirrored surface, exercised end-to-end (browser → Bun → ssh → remote agent → kill(pid)), zero kolu. That proof is the merge-gate for this kolu PR, not optional decoration: the return shape changed from Promise<void> to { procedures, done }, and drishti master still awaits the old shape on its streaming sinks (drishti #70 adopted the #1497 mirrorRemoteSurface, all CI green). So the paired drishti R7 PR #71 — audit that await to .done, pin drishti’s kolu source to this R7 revision, go green — is now open and gates #1505: its full CI is green against the new { procedures, done } API — all 18 odu nodes (every lane on both aarch64-darwin and x86_64-linux), the “Kill process” forwarded-procedure action landing on top of the migrated mirror — exactly the lane set #70 passed to gate #1497. Because that proof lives entirely in drishti’s own surface, R7 stands alone — it needs neither R6 nor kolu; R9 then consumes a proven total mirror for the remote dial. One caution this plan earlier blurred: the mirror (remote → local, proven here by drishti) is a different primitive from kolu composing-and-serving a stream to the browser over websocketLink + surfaceClient. That second leg is R-compose-2’s, and it is proven separately by pulam-web — not by this mirror. drishti proved the mirror; pulam-web proves the browser leg.

R8 — compose terminal-workspace into kolu-server (the fold vanishes)

R-compose-1· awareness — kolu reads (one writer per fact)◐ build-clean
needs ← R6blocks → R9links wake fix ✓ #1531
R-compose-2· git-status stream over ws○ todo
needs ← R6 · R-pulamweb-4blocks → R9links carry patch #1534

R6 left one impl, two contract shapes: kolu re-exposes fs/git on koluSurface’s own streams (in-process), pulam serves terminalWorkspaceSurface (remote). R8 collapses that to one surface — and settles the question the epic kept reopening, who owns awareness? The answer is not kolu.

Two moves, very different risk — a prototype (#1510, discarded as a branch) built both and reached full green CI, which tells us a lot. R-compose-1 — awareness: the one-writer-per-fact compose below. #1510 implemented it cleanly and it passed end-to-end, so it’s validated — a clean build from a working reference, not an open question. R-compose-2 — git-status over the ws: moving the Code tab’s git status onto terminalWorkspaceSurface’s {seq} watcher stream. #1510’s months-long fight here turned out not to be the transport (the pulse reached the browser fine) but the file-tree renderer not repainting under change-pulse churn — detail in its subsection. R-compose-2 still goes through pulam-web’s standalone graduation first (the drishti discipline), now sharpened to prove a live DOM repaint, not just data delivery. The architecture below is R-compose-1; R-compose-2 is its own subsection at the end.

A terminal is two things that were never one record:

  • authored state — kolu writes it, it is durable: id · restore-cwd · theme · layout · sleeping-state · the frozen-pr snapshot. Stays on koluSurface.
  • observed state — the sensors derive it, it is ephemeral and never persisted: cwd · git · pr · agent · foreground. Lives on terminalWorkspaceSurface.awareness.

The move: kolu stops holding the observation and reads it. The sensors write the terminalWorkspaceSurface.awareness collection — in-process locally — instead of mutating kolu’s terminalMetadata. kolu and the browser read it through one seam, awarenessFor(id): the in-process collection today, a host’s mirror in R9. terminalMetadata shrinks to kolu’s authored fields; kolu keeps a few restore-seeds (cwd · git-cache · agent-session) by sampling the observation at save time, so a cold restart re-spawns and re-resolves fast.

before (fold):  sensors ──┐
                          ├──►  terminalMetadata   (one co-owned record + the fence)
                kolu ─────┘

after (read):   kolu ────────►  koluSurface.terminalMetadata        (authored)
                sensors ─────►  terminalWorkspaceSurface.awareness  (observed)
                                kolu reads both via awarenessFor(id) — in-process local · mirror remote

The fence vanishes — it does not move. The persisted/live fence existed for one reason: the ~150 ms agent firehose shared terminalMetadata with must-persist fields, so a typed wall (updateServerLiveMetadata not arming the autosave) kept the churn off the session save. Once the sensors write the observation surface instead, kolu never persists the live fields at all — it subscribes only to cwd (human-cd frequency) for its restore-seed — so the firehose never touches kolu and the fence has nothing to guard. One writer per fact: the fold, the co-owned record, and the fence all delete. The “irreducible domain logic” the Architecture callout named was a held copy that only existed because, before pulam, the observation had nowhere else to live.

It ships local-only — the in-process surface already exists from R6 — so R8 stands without R9, and R9 becomes a pure backing swap behind awarenessFor. The three server-side readers of awareness today (exportTranscriptHtml, the active-agent count, the preview’s repo-root) move to that same seam — the rewiring a split-the-wire prototype would owe at R9 anyway, paid once, here.

trade-off
win · one writer per fact observation has one home; kolu authors only what it persists. The fold, the co-owned record, and the fence all delete — replaced by “compose two surfaces.” Adding a sensor field touches the workspace schema + a join-spread, never kolu’s wire.
win · local == remote the same seam answers both; the local/remote axis vanishes behind awarenessFor (in-process vs mirror). R9 is a backing swap, not a second data path.
cost · the surface moves the browser’s fs/git + awareness calls move under the terminalWorkspace namespace, and server-side readers move to awarenessFor — a one-time, one-repo change (R6 froze it deliberately; R8 unfreezes).
cost · awareness streams in after chrome the page paints identity, then live observation lands a beat later (seeded placeholder). Honest streaming — live presence legitimately follows static identity — not a torn-record artifact. A server restart shows awareness briefly blank until sensors/mirror catch up; a browser refresh is fine (snapshot-then-delta). The warm-vs-fresh trade kaval already took; theme/layout/location stay persisted.

Net (R-compose-1): the awareness compose deletes the fold, the fence, and the co-owned record in one move, and turns R9 into a backing swap. #1510 built and shipped this green — two collections joined fine-grained client-side, the awarenessFor seam (the R9 backing-swap point), a portable terminalMetadataFold.test.ts — so it’s a reference impl to port, not a design to re-derive. ✅ The one latent bug it caught — a no-op-upsert collection needs an explicit publish on every lifecycle flip (wake must re-publish, or a woken terminal stays showing the dormant body) — is now fixed on master (#1531), so R-compose-1 inherits a clean wake path.

R-compose-2 — the git-status stream (the gated move). The other half of “one surface” moves the Code tab’s git status off koluSurface onto terminalWorkspaceSurface — a read procedure (git.getStatus) refreshed by a {seq} watcher stream (subscribeRepoChange). #1510 got this green, so the transport is not the risk — the pulse reaches the browser. The risk that actually cost #1510 months is one layer up: the file-tree renderer. @pierre/trees swallows the first controller emit after every re-subscribe (and it re-subscribes on layout changes), so a live mutation landing in that window updates the model but never repaints the DOM — value right, screen stale. master’s old git-status stream dodged it only by fully remounting the tree; a persistently-mounted tree (the whole point of flicker-free live updates) exposes it. So R-compose-2 must carry the renderer fix, not just the data — and validation (2026-06-23) confirms it’s needed: unlike its deselect sibling #1533 (which beta.4 had already fixed upstream, now closed), the swallow-emit guard is still present in the pinned beta.4 — and beta.4 is the latest published version, so no upstream bump exists. Carry the vendored @pierre/trees patch (always bump the revision on every emit) + the client store written via reconcile (a no-op on no-change, so no remount-flood) — both lifted from #1510 (useWatchedRead). That work graduates standalone in pulam-web first, whose hermetic test asserts a DOM repaint of a mounted tree under a same-shape change, not just a store re-notify. Then R-compose-2 is a backing-swap onto proven electricity. (Design fork, closed: the consumer can be value-bearing or the {seq}-pulse-plus-requery — #1510 proved the pulse shape green; either way the renderer is what must repaint.)

R9 — kolu dials remotes

R9· kolu dials remotes○ todo
needs ← R7 · R-compose-2blocks → R10

The linchpin — it needs R7 (the total mirror) and is blocked-on R-compose-2 (the composed surface + its per-terminal dispatch; R-compose-2 proves the browser-ws-stream leg R9 leans on). kolu-server gains the N-endpoint registry + the ssh driver — the {kind:"remote",host} arm, the (host,id) join, a persisted location — reusing the same getHostSession the spike (R3) built. It dials the host’s kaval for the PTY (via the shipped frontDaemonOverStdio) → a remote tile; and dials its pulam over a long-lived HostSession, mirroring the whole terminal-workspace surface (R7) into one local handle. Because R8 already made that surface the one kolu serves the browser, R9 just points the remote terminal’s dispatch at the mirror instead of the in-process impl. No fold to write (R8 dissolved it), no second data path. The mirror’s streams — awareness activity + the fs/git {seq} watchers — re-serve to the browser over the same websocketLink + surfaceClient leg R-compose-2 proves (and R9 turns the quiet activity stream live on it), so R-compose-2’s pulam-web graduations gate R9, not just R8. location is persisted kolu-side, re-validated on reconnect against the remote kaval’s live cwd. (Paste/upload is a TerminalEndpoint concern — the same seam, the PRECONDITION_FAILED guard.) The result: a remote terminal as capable as a local one — PTY, awareness badge, green dot, working Code tab. The shape is the lead diagram above.

Adoption — a dial joins, never clobbers. If the host already runs a kaval it is adopted (the same adoptOrEnsure spine the local boot uses); a duplicate is impossible by construction (the pid-gate’s atomic link(2) EEXIST). On dial:

the remote kaval is… kolu does
absent provision + spawn fresh (adoptOrEnsure → ensure)
live · wire-compatible adopt — connect, never kill; its PTYs reconcile in
live · a build behind adopt + an “update pending” nudge (orthogonal)
live · contract-skewed recycle (kill → respawn) — only on a typed DaemonContractSkewError
unreachable (bad dial) bounded retry, then degraded — never killed

One kaval per remote host: dialing joins its terminals (“the prod terminals”), never racing to kill them.

R10 — canvas multiplex + validate

R10· canvas multiplex + validate○ todo
needs ← R9

The UI: an active-kaval strip (local always present), a per-tile host hint, an ssh-config host picker — kinda like tmux sessions. Then validate the feel on real use: does N-kavals-at-once beat tmux-style one-at-a-time switching? Fall back to switch if multiplex doesn’t earn its keep. The granularity call lands here, validated, not up front.

History

  • 2026-05-26 → 28 — R1 shipped: the TerminalEndpoint seam (#981), the framework gaps + @kolu/surface-nix-host (#984), and the shared sensor engine (#1004).
  • R2 re-sequenced before R3/R5 — a whole-plan /lowy pass showed that building the multi-client survivor once collapses the later phases; R2 split into pty-daemon and shipped end-to-end (R2.1 → R2.5), closing #671 (terminals survive a deploy).
  • Multi-host end state set 2026-06-11: 1 local + N ssh kavals, host-switched in the ChromeBar; R2’s redo baked the host-count-agnostic shapes (hostId-keyed status, the HostLocation discriminator) so R3 retrofits nothing.
  • Renumbered to one R1–R9 tree (2026-06-21) — shipped branches R1–R4 (was R-1/R-1.5/R-4/R-2/R-3 + colliding child P/B schemes), and the remaining work lifted out of the child notes into a linear R5–R9 roadmap defined here (so the forward plan lives in one place; fs/git joins awareness in one library, @kolu/terminal-workspace, as its own phase R6; completing mirrorRemoteSurface to a total dual got its own phase R7 — proven via drishti’s “Kill process” action, so it stands alone, before kolu’s fold (R8) depends on it). Git holds the old labels. Cf. ghostex vs kolu, herdr vs kolu, kaval vs zmosh.
  • R6 shipped (2026-06-21) — @kolu/terminal-awareness grew into @kolu/terminal-workspace: one thin fs/git wrapper (createTerminalWorkspaceEndpoint) lifted out of kolu-server’s localEndpoint, re-exposed through two deliberately different contract shapes — kolu-server keeps it in-process on koluSurface’s value-bearing streams, while pulam serves it on terminalWorkspaceSurface as fs/git read procedures + {seq} change-pulse watcher streams (that surface’s contract 0.2 → 0.3). One impl, not one shared surface — the single surface both homes serve is mirrored by kolu in R8. @kolu/pulam-contract retired into the library. Done ahead of R5 (the phases are independent). #1506
  • R7 shipped (2026-06-21, #1505) — mirrorRemoteSurface returns { procedures, done }: streaming primitives fold into the sink as before, every procedure comes back as a forwarding stub (procedures.<ns>.<verb>(input) → runs on the remote, returns). serve ∘ mirror ≈ identity is pinned by an in-repo unit test. The breaking return-shape change rode surface.md’s drishti merge-gate: the paired drishti PR #71 (audited drishti’s await mirrorRemoteSurface(...) → .done and pinned drishti’s kolu source to this R7 revision) went green across all 18 odu nodes (every lane on both aarch64-darwin and x86_64-linux), the same gate #70 passed for #1497. R9’s remote dial consumes the total mirror — R8 having composed it into the one surface kolu serves.
  • One-surface phase injected (2026-06-22) — a new R8 composes terminalWorkspaceSurface into the one surface kolu serves the browser and dissolves the server-side awareness fold; the former kolu dials remotes and canvas phases shift to R9 · R10. The tree is now R1–R10. Trade-offs in the R8 section.
  • R8 sharpened to one-writer-per-fact (2026-06-22) — a prototype (#1510, “Shape A”) split awareness onto its own wire surface but kept the co-owned record server-side, so the fold and the fence survived as internals. A Hickey/Lowy pass (and the prototype’s own adversary) showed the record was never one thing: R8 now has the sensors write the observation surface so kolu reads awareness through one seam (awarenessFor) rather than holding a copy — the fold, the co-owned record, and the fence all delete, R8 ships local-only, and R9 becomes a backing swap. The merge is not “a client-side join of a torn record”; it is honest composition of two distinct concerns (authored vs observed). The linchpin that makes it cheap: the live fields already never reach disk (snapshotSession strips them), so there is no on-disk migration.
  • R8 split into R8a/R8b; the fs/git stream-transport gap found (2026-06-22) — #1510 validated R8a (awareness) end-to-end but stuck on 9 e2e failures, all the fs/git Code-tab live updates: the surface’s {seq} watcher stream not driving the browser over websocketLink + surfaceClient (procedures + the snapshot cross fine; the async delta doesn’t). Verification (ground-truth + adversary) killed the “framework publisher bridging” guess — surface streams carry no publisher channel, and the working/broken streams are byte-identical siblings — leaving the source-arm {seq} + client-reconcile path as the genuinely-new, never-graduated primitive. So the plan now splits R8a (architecture, validated) from R8b (the stream-transport move) and treats R8b the way R7 treated drishti: prove it in a standalone consumer first. That work branches into pulam as R4.6 (the rename — arivu → pulam, kept small), R4.7 (pulam-tui grows a live fs/git view — the {seq} shape) and R4.8 (pulam-web — kolu’s exact ws + surfaceClient + reconcile leg). #1510 is kept as the reference that validated R8a and surfaced the gap; R8b’s clean compose lands on twice-proven electricity. R9 is blocked-on R8b (it re-serves the mirror’s streams over the same leg).
  • Renumbered + #1510 reviewed before discard (2026-06-23) — (1) Scheme: phases now read as a lineage — multi-phase sub-projects flatten to R-<name>-<n> (R-pulamweb-1/2/3, R-compose-1/2), killing the floating Step 0 and the alpha R8a/R8b; the R1–R10 spine stays. (2) #1510 reviewed: the prototype reached full green CI, correcting the entry above — its months-long block was not the stream transport (the {seq} pulse reached the browser fine) but the file-tree renderer (@pierre/trees) failing to repaint under change-pulse churn. So R-compose-1’s awareness compose is a working reference impl (upgraded to “build-clean”), and R-compose-2’s real risk is repainting a mounted tree live — carry #1510’s @pierre/trees patch + useWatchedRead. A latent wake-publish bug it caught is filed as #1529. #1510 the branch is discarded; its insights live in the roadmap.
  • Wake fixed · deselect obsolete · Pierre salvage is version-specific (2026-06-23) — the latent wake-publish bug (#1529) is now fixed on master (#1531), so R-compose-1 inherits a clean wake path. The deselect-before-remove salvage (#1533) was investigated and closed obsolete — Pierre beta.4 already detaches a selected node; the real lingering (a committed file rm’d from the working tree stays, because browse lists git’s index ∪ untracked) is a separate product call, now #1536. The lesson: #1510’s @pierre/trees salvage is version-specific, so R-pulamweb-4 / R-compose-2 must validate the swallow-emit (#1534) against current beta.4 before carrying the patch — not assume it.
  • R-dock-unify shipped (2026-06-23, #1541) — kolu’s Dock became the third consumer of the shared @kolu/terminal-workspace/agentProjection, reached through the client’s existing kolu-common/surface door (a re-export, not a new client → terminal-workspace edge). Its needs-you ranking now derives from agentUrgency + URGENCY_RANK (so a just-finished waiting agent ranks idle, matching pulam-web), its tile-paint fold (dockModel.paintBucket) reads the shared agentPaintClass — which paints waiting as awaiting so the glow lingers until park — the orphaned isWorkingState/WorkingState (and the whole agentState.ts module) are deleted, and a cross-consumer differential test pins Dock urgency ≡ agentProjection across every state — the test the #1535 review flagged as absent. The alert layer (useTerminalAlerts) deliberately keeps treating waiting as notify-worthy: “notify me something happened” is a different question from “rank by what needs my action,” so it classifies waiting differently on purpose.
  • R-pulamweb-4 gating question resolved (2026-06-23) — validated the @pierre/trees renderer against the pinned beta.4 before building (the #1533 lesson: don’t assume the #1510 salvage still applies). Result: the swallow-emit guard (hasSeenInitialControllerSnapshot in FileTreeView.js) is still present in beta.4, and beta.4 is the latest published version — so unlike the deselect bug there is no upstream fix, and the #1534 patch is required, not speculative. R-pulamweb-4 is now build-ready: carry the patch (delete the guard so every emit bumps the revision) + a reconcile-into-store write, render the tree through @kolu/solid-pierre (kolu’s Code-tab wrapper), and pin it with a hermetic test driving a same-shape mutation right after a re-subscribe.
  • pulam-web state-staleness fixed; upstream Pierre issue filed (2026-06-24) — a root-cause hunt into a pulam-web ↔ Dock divergence (an opencode agent shown working in pulam-web but idle in the Dock) found the cause: pulam-web’s re-serve never cleared its awareness cache on an ssh-link respawn, so a working→idle during a link-down window stayed pinned (the Dock reads kolu’s own in-process awareness, so it stayed correct). Fixed in #1550 — the foundation R-pulamweb-4 builds on. Separately, the @pierre/trees swallow-emit bug R-pulamweb-4 patches is now filed upstream as pierrecomputer/pierre#883 (the package is Preact), so the vendored patch can be dropped once Pierre publishes a fix.
  • R-pip-unify shipped (2026-06-23, #1551) — the visual sequel to R-dock-unify. The Dock’s StatePip + PipVariant lifted into a new presentation leaf @kolu/solid-statepip that both kolu’s Dock and pulam-web render, so a given agent state shows the identical pip (glyph · colour · animation). The agent-paint → pip mapping lives once (pipForPaintClass), folded through by both surfaces — drift is impossible, not just test-pinned. Colour converged via a new CSS-only @kolu/theme package both surfaces @import: pulam-web is brought onto the shared Tailwind theme (class="dark"), retiring its render-local amber/cyan palette for the Dock’s “your turn = violet” semantics, and its hand-rolled PAINT glyph table is deleted. pulam-web inherits the sleeping ☾ and never emits the Dock-only unread attention. The @theme-in-@import + cross-package @source CSS pipeline was verified by a real build before the gauntlet.

Referenced by

  • Ghostex vs. kolu remote-terminals
  • Kaval sessions — dial daemons, multiplex the canvas
  • pulam — the terminal-workspace surface as an ephemeral daemon on kaval
  • pulam-web — the browser twin (kolu's surface-consumption leg, proven standalone)
  • R2 — kaval, the standalone PTY daemon
← the Atlasgithub ↗
